atmosphere.mood: Quiet and contemplative. Candles flicker on stone walls. Hushed voices conceal more than they reveal.
atmosphere.lighting: Candlelit — small tallow candles on every table, iron sconces along the walls
atmosphere.smells: Beeswax, dried herbs, faint incense, and bread baking somewhere beyond the door
atmosphere.sounds: Murmured prayers, the scratch of quill on parchment, distant bells, the occasional cough

layout: A stone-walled room attached to a small abbey. Wooden benches line trestle tables worn smooth by generations of pilgrims. A simple bar of plain timber serves the room. A heavy door at the far end leads to the cloister, always slightly ajar.

drinks.name: Monastery ale
drinks.description: Brewed by the brothers — light, slightly bitter, blessed (allegedly)

drinks.name: Herb tisane
drinks.description: Dried chamomile and mint steeped in hot water

drinks.name: Small beer
drinks.description: Low in strength, high in virtue

drinks.name: Monastery wine
drinks.description: A careful vintage from the abbey's own vineyard — dark, dry, and solemn

drinks.name: Rosehip cordial
drinks.description: A sweet non-alcoholic cordial pressed from hedgerow berries — refreshing and restorative

drinks.name: Elder spirit
drinks.description: A small medicinal measure of strong spirit distilled by the apothecary brother

drinks.name: Water
drinks.description: A cup of well water, blessed by the abbot

food.name: Brown bread
food.description: Dense and filling — baked fresh each morning

food.name: Pottage
food.description: Thick vegetable broth with barley and herbs

food.name: Hard cheese
food.description: Aged in the cellar, crumbling but sharp

food.name: Vegetable pottage
food.description: A heartier version of the standard pottage — rich with garden vegetables and monastery herbs

food.name: Honey cakes
food.description: Small dense cakes sweetened with monastery honey — a rare treat offered to pious guests
